warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui-reactivity analysis of pyridine (user-supplied pyridine.xyz, 11 atoms, used as-is — single-point, no geometry relaxation) computed with DFT at tier 'standard' = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (functional and basis set explicitly, exact four-center integrals, density fitting off) in PySCF, gas phase. Parent state charge 0, multiplicity 1 (RKS); cation state charge +1, multiplicity 2 (UKS doublet); anion state charge -1, multiplicity 2 (UKS doublet). Condensed Fukui functions derived from finite differences of Mulliken (PySCF) atomic charges.

Headline result: the most electrophilic site is atom 1 (N, ring nitrogen) with f+ = 0.48423. The most nucleophilic site is atom 4 (C) with f- = 0.11826. The dual descriptor is largest and positive at nitrogen (0.42010), confirming it as the dominant electrophilic center.

Integrity: status ok, trustworthy = true. All three integrity checks passed (ok=true): sum(f+) = 1.0000 and sum(f-) = 1.0000 (charge conservation), and common header present. There were no true warning-severity failures; the check detail strings are surfaced verbatim in the warnings field for transparency.

Reproducibility: cli.py fukui .../pyridine.xyz --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults. Result JSON at /home/erobles/pyridine_fukui_dft.json; bar-chart PNG at .../20260704-192054_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. No experimental/literature comparison offered (none requested).
